data.body: BLOCKER ASSESSMENT (2026-07-17, agent): E5 is BLOCKED on R5 (ea39, status=open, NOT landed) — cannot be completed independently. Evidence: (1) all 4 of E5's ACs reference R5's candidate change — AC1 'runs the amended kernel', AC2 'diff EMPTY: byte-identical (decision,priority,validity)' is inherently baseline-vs-amended, AC3 'the new sub-answer is na outside the G6/E4/T3 cohort'. (2) R5's amendment is absent from code: grep 'asserted_capability_confirmed' src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ returns nothing. (3) Scope explicitly reads 'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (candidate branch build)' — that candidate branch does not exist yet. Unlike E2/E3 (harness+corpus+baseline, candidate later), E5 IS the candidate-vs-baseline diff: a self-vs-self replay of today's kernel would trivially be empty and prove nothing, and would FAIL the completion verifier (AC3 cites a sub-answer absent from the codebase). Spend note: E5 itself is LOW-spend when unblocked (deterministic replay of pass-3 math in src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py — validity()/priority=validity*impact/decision core; plus a ~20-finding live sanity slice). Corpus docs/experiments/code_review_adjudication.jsonl (161 lines) is present. RECOMMENDATION: schedule E5 immediately AFTER R5's candidate change exists on a branch; leave E5 open/unclaimed until then.
